SEARCH
Package org.unlaxer.tramli

Class DataFlowGraph<S extends Enum<S> & FlowState>

java.lang.Object
org.unlaxer.tramli.DataFlowGraph<S>
public final class DataFlowGraph<S extends Enum<S> & FlowState> extends Object
Bipartite graph of data types and processors/guards derived from a FlowDefinition. Built automatically during FlowDefinition.build().

Type nodes represent data types (Class<?>). Processing nodes represent processors, guards, or branch processors. Edges are "requires" (type → processing) and "produces" (processing → type).

    Diff two data-flow graphs.

    static final record 
    DataFlowGraph.Impact<S>
    Impact analysis: all producers and consumers of a given type.
    static final record 
    DataFlowGraph.Lifetime<S>
    Data lifetime: which states a type is first produced and last consumed.
    static final record 
    DataFlowGraph.MissingInfo<S>
    Info about a missing type at a state.
    static final record 
    DataFlowGraph.NodeInfo<S>
    Info about a processor/guard that produces or consumes a type.
    static final record 
    DataFlowGraph.ProducerInfo<S>
    Info about a producer: its name and the state where it produces the type.

  • Method Details

    • availableAt

      public Set<Class<?>> availableAt(S state)
      Data types available in context when the flow reaches the given state.

    • producersOf

      public List<DataFlowGraph.NodeInfo<S>> producersOf(Class<?> type)
      Processors/guards that produce the given type.

    • consumersOf

      public List<DataFlowGraph.NodeInfo<S>> consumersOf(Class<?> type)
      Processors/guards that consume (require) the given type.

    • deadData

      public Set<Class<?>> deadData()
      Types that are produced but never required by any downstream processor/guard.

    • lifetime

      public DataFlowGraph.Lifetime<S> lifetime(Class<?> type)
      Get the lifetime of a type across the flow. Null if type is not in the graph.

    • pruningHints

      public Map<S,Set<Class<?>>> pruningHints()
      Context pruning hints: for each state, types that are available but not required by any processor/guard at that state or any state reachable from it. These types could theoretically be removed from context at that state.

    • isCompatible

      public static boolean isCompatible(StateProcessor a, StateProcessor b)
      Check if processor B can replace processor A without breaking data-flow. B is compatible with A if: B requires no more than A, and B produces at least what A produces.

    • verifyProcessor

      public static List<String> verifyProcessor(StateProcessor processor, FlowContext ctx)
      Verify that a processor's declared requires are available in context, and after execution, its declared produces are present. Returns list of violations (empty = OK).

    • allTypes

      public Set<Class<?>> allTypes()
      All type nodes in the graph.

    • assertDataFlow

      public List<Class<?>> assertDataFlow(FlowContext ctx, S currentState)
      Assert that a flow instance's current context satisfies the data-flow invariant. Every type in availableAt(currentState) must be present in the context. Returns list of missing types (empty = OK).

    • explain

      public DataFlowGraph.ExplainResult<S> explain(S state)
      Explain data availability at a state. For each missing type, trace why it is unavailable. Checks all types required by outgoing processors/guards/branches from that state.

    • explain

      public DataFlowGraph.ExplainResult<S> explain(S state, Class<?> key)
      Explain data availability at a state. If key is non-null, only that type is checked. Otherwise all types required by outgoing transitions from the state are checked.

    • whyMissing

      public List<String> whyMissing(Class<?> key, S state)
      Human-readable explanation of why a type is missing at a state. Returns a list of explanation strings.

    • impactOf

      public DataFlowGraph.Impact<S> impactOf(Class<?> type)

    • parallelismHints

      public List<String[]> parallelismHints()
      Parallelism hints: pairs of processors at the same state that have no data dependency (neither requires the other's produces).

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Structured JSON representation of the data-flow graph.

    • toRenderable

      public RenderableGraph.DataFlow toRenderable()
      Convert to a renderable view for pluggable renderers.

    • renderDataFlow

      public String renderDataFlow(Function<RenderableGraph.DataFlow,String> renderer)
      Render the data-flow graph using a custom renderer.

    • toMermaid

      public String toMermaid()
      Generate Mermaid data-flow diagram (shortcut for renderDataFlow(MermaidGenerator::dataFlow)).

    • migrationOrder

      public List<String> migrationOrder()
      Recommended migration order: processors sorted by dependency count (fewest first). Processors with no requires come first, then those whose requires are all produced by earlier processors.

    • toMarkdown

      public String toMarkdown()
      Generate a Markdown migration checklist.

    • testScaffold

      public Map<String,List<String>> testScaffold()
      Test scaffold: for each processor/guard, list the types needed in context (requires). Returns a map of processor name → required type names.

    • generateInvariantAssertions

      public List<String> generateInvariantAssertions()
      Generate data-flow invariant assertions as strings. Each assertion: "At state X: context must contain [A, B, C]"

    • crossFlowMap

      public static List<String> crossFlowMap(DataFlowGraph<?>... graphs)
      Cross-flow data-flow map: find types that flow A produces and flow B requires (or vice versa). Returns pairs of (type, producerFlow, consumerFlow).

    • diff

      public static DataFlowGraph.DiffResult diff(DataFlowGraph<?> before, DataFlowGraph<?> after)

    • versionCompatibility

      public static <S extends Enum<S> & FlowState> List<String> versionCompatibility(DataFlowGraph<S> before, DataFlowGraph<S> after)
      Version compatibility: check if instances running on defBefore can resume on defAfter. Returns list of incompatibilities per state.
